check: Java and Spring Boot versions are explicit and compatible with the selected supported target
evidence_required: Build descriptor with Java and Spring Boot versions plus target support reference
recommended_action: Select a supported target pair and make compiler and runtime versions explicit

check: Dependency and build-plugin versions are controlled, convergent, and inventory-ready
evidence_required: Parsed dependency and plugin inventory with convergence evidence
recommended_action: Centralize versions, remove duplicate overrides, and resolve dependency convergence failures

check: External configuration is type-bound, validated, and fails fast when required values are invalid
evidence_required: Configuration classes, validation constraints, and startup validation tests
recommended_action: Replace scattered property lookups with validated typed configuration

check: JVM, container, executor, thread-pool, and connection-pool settings are explicit
evidence_required: Versioned runtime flags and bounded resource configuration
recommended_action: Declare resource settings and avoid environment-dependent implicit defaults

check: Source code is free of removed, deprecated-for-removal, and internal JDK APIs for the selected target
evidence_required: Target-aware compiler, jdeprscan, or equivalent static-analysis output
recommended_action: Replace unsupported JDK APIs before changing the runtime

check: Source and configuration do not rely on deprecated Spring APIs, mechanisms, or properties for the selected target
evidence_required: Spring migration report and source locations for deprecated usage
recommended_action: Apply each skipped migration guide and remove temporary migration helpers

check: Namespaces, annotations, bytecode tools, and library integrations are compatible with the target stack
evidence_required: Compatibility inventory covering imports, annotation processors, agents, and framework integrations
recommended_action: Upgrade or replace incompatible integrations before the framework change

check: Framework extension points and default-sensitive behavior are inventoried as migration hotspots
evidence_required: Inventory of filters, interceptors, serializers, converters, proxies, aspects, and custom auto-configuration
recommended_action: Review each extension point against target release notes and make critical defaults explicit

check: Package or module boundaries and dependency direction are explicit and enforceable
evidence_required: Architecture test or module descriptor showing allowed dependencies
recommended_action: Define boundaries and enforce them with architecture tests

check: Business logic is separated from web, persistence, messaging, and framework-specific code
evidence_required: Representative dependency paths and boundary tests
recommended_action: Move business decisions behind framework-neutral application or domain interfaces

check: External systems are accessed through stable interfaces or adapters
evidence_required: Adapter inventory for HTTP, messaging, filesystem, and vendor SDK access
recommended_action: Isolate external clients so integrations can be upgraded and tested independently

check: Circular dependencies, service locators, static application-context access, and hidden framework coupling are absent
evidence_required: Static-analysis results for dependency cycles and framework-context access
recommended_action: Replace hidden coupling with constructor-injected explicit dependencies

check: Database migrations are versioned, additive, and backward-compatible
evidence_required: Migration scripts and expand-contract review evidence
recommended_action: Separate additive and destructive changes across a compatibility window

check: Entity mapping, fetch strategy, cascade behavior, locking, and schema generation are explicit
evidence_required: Persistence mappings and tests for relationship and locking behavior
recommended_action: Remove risky implicit ORM behavior and document intentional mappings

check: Transaction boundaries, propagation, isolation, rollback rules, and timeouts are explicit
evidence_required: Transaction annotations or configuration plus rollback tests
recommended_action: Place transaction boundaries in the service layer and configure non-default semantics deliberately

check: Queries, pagination, batching, and bulk operations have explicit bounds
evidence_required: Repository queries and tests demonstrating bounded result and batch sizes
recommended_action: Add pagination or batching and prevent unbounded data loading

check: Exceptions are classified and are not swallowed or converted into false success
evidence_required: Exception-handling paths and negative tests
recommended_action: Preserve failure semantics and remove catch-all success responses

check: Remote calls use deadlines, bounded retries, failure isolation, and operation-safe idempotency
evidence_required: Client configuration and failure-injection tests
recommended_action: Set connect and response deadlines and retry only safe transient failures

check: Message consumers handle duplicates, poison messages, dead letters, and replay
evidence_required: Consumer configuration and duplicate, dead-letter, and replay tests
recommended_action: Make handlers idempotent and define bounded recovery paths

check: Async tasks, schedulers, executors, and mutable shared state are safe and bounded
evidence_required: Executor configuration and concurrency-focused tests
recommended_action: Use bounded executors and remove unsafe shared mutable state

check: Secrets and credentials are absent from source and packaged configuration
evidence_required: Secret-scan output and externalized configuration evidence
recommended_action: Remove and rotate confirmed credentials and use an external secret source

check: Authentication and authorization protect every exposed boundary
evidence_required: Security configuration, access-control mapping, and negative tests
recommended_action: Enforce endpoint and method authorization with deny-by-default behavior

check: Untrusted input is validated before SQL, file, deserialization, command, log, or response sinks
evidence_required: Validation, encoding, redaction, and security-test evidence
recommended_action: Validate at trust boundaries and prevent injection or sensitive-data disclosure

check: Dependencies and build plugins can be checked for vulnerabilities and supply-chain risk
evidence_required: Dependency inventory, vulnerability scan, and SBOM configuration
recommended_action: Automate component analysis and review actionable findings

check: Critical business behavior has automated regression coverage
evidence_required: Critical-behavior test map and executable tests
recommended_action: Protect current behavior before refactoring or upgrading

check: Persistence and integration behavior is tested with representative infrastructure
evidence_required: Integration tests using production-compatible disposable dependencies
recommended_action: Test real framework integration instead of relying only on mocks

check: API and message compatibility is verified automatically
evidence_required: Consumer, provider, schema, or compatibility test results
recommended_action: Run compatibility tests before changing public contracts

check: Tests are deterministic, isolated, and independent of execution order
evidence_required: Repeat-run results and test-isolation configuration
recommended_action: Remove shared state, timing assumptions, and environment-dependent test behavior

check: Logs are structured, correlated, and exclude sensitive data
evidence_required: Logging configuration, correlation propagation, and redaction tests
recommended_action: Use structured events and propagate correlation context across boundaries

check: Metrics instrument requests, dependencies, messaging, and executors
evidence_required: Meter definitions and Actuator or Micrometer configuration
recommended_action: Expose traffic, error, latency, and saturation signals at code boundaries

check: Trace context propagates across HTTP, messaging, and asynchronous boundaries
evidence_required: Tracing dependencies, propagation configuration, and instrumentation points
recommended_action: Preserve trace context across every asynchronous or remote boundary

check: Health, readiness, and startup state reflect application and dependency conditions
evidence_required: Health indicators, readiness configuration, and startup-state tests
recommended_action: Report readiness only after required application state is usable

check: Method, class, and module complexity is controlled
evidence_required: Static-analysis output for complexity and oversized code units
recommended_action: Split high-complexity code along cohesive responsibilities

check: Duplication, dead code, and unused dependencies are minimized
evidence_required: Duplicate-code, dead-code, and unused-dependency analysis
recommended_action: Remove unused paths and consolidate duplicated behavior before migration

check: Technical-debt markers and suppressions are scoped and actionable
evidence_required: Inventory of TODO, FIXME, and suppression locations with rationale
recommended_action: Replace broad suppressions and convert migration debt into explicit actions

check: Static-analysis rules are versioned and broad suppressions are avoided
evidence_required: Checkstyle, PMD, SpotBugs, Error Prone, or equivalent configuration
recommended_action: Adopt enforceable rules and keep exclusions narrow and documented

check: HTTP APIs and message schemas have machine-readable contracts
evidence_required: OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, GraphQL, Avro, Protobuf, or JSON Schema artifacts
recommended_action: Version contract artifacts with the code that implements them

check: Request, response, and event changes preserve backward compatibility
evidence_required: Old-new contract comparison and compatibility evidence
recommended_action: Prefer additive evolution and retain compatibility during the migration window

check: Versioning, status codes, error models, and deprecation behavior are consistent
evidence_required: Controller or handler review plus documented error and version policy
recommended_action: Standardize public error semantics and make deprecation explicit

check: Serialization rules for dates, enums, nulls, and unknown fields are explicit
evidence_required: Serializer configuration and edge-case compatibility tests
recommended_action: Make wire-format behavior explicit and tolerant where compatibility requires it
